plan_metadata.name: Feature LocalDebug Playground Symbol Link Target Already Exist

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" dialog.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click on the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface at coordinates (288, 101).
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
base_path="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_C7YUN"
new_folder="$base_path/devTools/playground"

if [ -d "$base_path" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$new_folder"
  echo "Folder created: $new_folder"
else
  echo "Base path does not exist: $base_path"
  exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Folder created: /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_C7YUN/devTools/playground

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code create a new folder devTools/playground under file /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}
@assertion  'devTools' folder is visible
Click the browser tab close button (red crosshair at top-right corner) in the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web application running on localhost.
Click the "Explorer" icon in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code interface to open the file explorer panel.
Click the "devTools" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand or collapse its contents.
Click the "playground" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code, under the "VSCAUSE_APP_C7YUN" workspace, to expand or select its contents.
@assertion 'playground' folder has a subfolder named 'node_modules'
@code copy file #text:/home/vscode/.fx/telemetryTest.log to  /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/index.js
Click the "index.js" file in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to open it for editing within the "VSCUSE_APP_P4PXC" workspace, under the "playground" folder.

Click the mouse at (651, 237) to place the cursor within the `index.js` file's editor pane in Visual Studio Code, specifically on the line containing `"undefined"`, allowing direct editing of code at that position.
Open the in-file search bar in the "index.js" editor tab within Visual Studio Code by pressing **Ctrl+F**.
Click the "Find" input field in the search bar at the top of the Visual Studio Code editor window, targeting the text entry box for searching within the file 'index.js'.
Type the text 'TelemTest - debug-all' into the Find input box in the Visual Studio Code editor while editing the file 'index.js'.
@assertion '[DEBUG] TelemTest - debug-all-start ' is visible
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal, within the "Debug Console" panel, to execute the currently typed command.
@assertion '[DEBUG] TelemTest - debug-all ' is visible
